mnemonics: NASH - No Advantage in Switching Here: A Nash Equilibrium is reached when no player can benefit by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming the others' strategies remain fixed.
PNE (Pure Nash Equilibrium): Remember it as a 'Pure & Narrow Equilibrium' – it's a specific, single strategy chosen by each player, not a mix of strategies.
keyPoints: A strategic game is a model of interactive decision-making with multiple players.
Each player has a set of possible strategies and a payoff function that depends on the strategies chosen by all players.
A Nash Equilibrium is a fundamental solution concept where no player can improve their payoff by changing their strategy alone.
commonMistakes: Assuming every game has a Pure Nash Equilibrium (PNE). Many games, like 'Matching Pennies', do not have a PNE and require mixed strategies.
Confusing a dominant strategy (best no matter what others do) with a Nash Equilibrium strategy (best only if others stick to their equilibrium strategy).
